text.title: Tailwind CSS — Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules every tailwind application must obey.

rule.statement: Declare all design tokens (colors, spacing, typography, radii, shadows) in tailwind.config theme.extend. Never hardcode values in components.
rule.rationale: Single source of truth; changing a token updates everywhere; prevents drift.

rule.statement: Use a cn() helper that combines clsx + tailwind-merge for conditional classes. Never raw template strings with conditional class concatenation.
rule.rationale: tailwind-merge resolves conflicts (px-2 px-4 → px-4); raw strings ship both and CSS specificity decides arbitrarily.

rule.statement: Use @apply only inside the utility layer for genuinely reusable patterns. Do not @apply for a single component's styles.
rule.rationale: @apply for one-off pulls Tailwind back into custom CSS; loses the utility-first benefit.

rule.statement: Apply utilities mobile-first; use sm:/md:/lg:/xl: prefixes to scale up.
rule.rationale: Tailwind responsive utilities are min-width based; mobile-first is the design grain.

rule.statement: Wire dark mode via CSS-variable token strategy (darkMode: 'class', tokens reference CSS vars).
rule.rationale: CSS variable strategy lets one token definition serve both themes; class strategy avoids prefers-color-scheme fragility.
